{{Infobox character
| name = Valinhall Incarnation
| aliases = Incarnation of Valinhall
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Valinhall Incarnation, Incarnation of Valinhall
| abilities = shadows that kidnap people, calling black chains
| introduced = tg-02-crimson-vault
}}

''See also: [[Valinhall Incarnation Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Valinhall]] [[Incarnation]] is a powerful entity and an extension of the [[Valinhall]] [[Territory]], which was established sixty years ago through the experiments of [[Damasca]]. This entity represents a [[Traveler]]'s power that has become uncontrolled and rampant, driven by personal grief or ambition. The [[Incarnation]] functions as a natural force that seeks to transform the world into a harsh, combat-oriented environment where status is earned through battle. Its presence can physically warp the surrounding landscape, causing objects to transform into different forms as the world attempts to mimic [[Valinhall]]. The original founder of the territory was a man named [[Valin]], who used his power to turn the territory against [[Damasca]]. While most travelers were lost in that conflict, leaving only a few survivors, the concept of an [[Incarnation]] remains a significant threat. An [[Incarnation]] can be a destructive force, such as one that escaped imprisonment to go on a rampage, destroying villages and marching toward specific targets like royal palaces. These entities are often bound to their territory and may face death or full incarnation if they fall too deep into debt with it. Though typically seen as a manifestation of power gone wrong, the relationship between founders and incarnations is complex, with some suggesting that being both could create a unique level of power.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|After escaping from his prison, the [[Valinhall]] [[Incarnation]] arrived at the palace as a dark and violent entity consumed by ambition. This fast-moving figure, characterized by black eyes and a body wrapped in shadow chains, wielded a gold-and-silver [[sword]] while utilizing shadows to kidnap people. During the ensuing battle, he served as a planned distraction force and engaged in combat against both [[Kai]] and [[Simon]]. While [[Alin]] suspected [[Simon]] was attempting to kill the creature, [[Simon]] fought specifically to slay him. Ultimately, this horrible being could be unleashed upon [[Zakareth]] and faced potential challenges from those who summon the mask.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] has escaped his prison.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c9">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Alin]] suspects [[Simon]] is trying to kill the [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is located in the palace.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is described as dark, horrible, violent, and capable of having shadows that kidnap people.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is likely to be challenged by those who summon the mask.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] has been consumed and warped by ambition.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] can be unleashed on [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is part of a planned distraction force.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] was fighting [[Kai]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] was involved in the battle.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is a man with black eyes whose body is wrapped in the shadows of chains.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is a fast-moving figure with a gold-and-silver [[sword]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Simon]] was fighting to slay the [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Valinhall]] [[Incarnation]] is a type of entity or power capable of transforming environments and objects through its mere presence. This scary force was once fought by [[Leah's father]] and has been known to issue orders from behind [[Zakareth]]. While [[Caela]] sensed an [[Incarnation]] outside the Gate, it is revealed that [[Indirial]] serves as the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]]. [[Simon]] eventually becomes the [[Valinhall]] [[Incarnation]] by wearing a specific mask, and he remains the only individual to successfully defeat the last incarnation. Additionally, this entity may have been involved in the summoning of black chains.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]]'s presence can transform objects and environments.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c1">''City of Light'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah's father]] fought the [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c7">''City of Light'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is described as being scary.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Wearing the mask causes [[Simon]] to become the [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is the [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] is the only one to win a fight against the last [[Valinhall Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Incarnation]] of [[Valinhall]] was giving orders behind [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Caela]] felt a [[Valinhall Incarnation]] outside the Gate.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Valinhall Incarnation]] is a type of entity/power.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|A [[Valinhall Incarnation]] may have been involved in calling black chains.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“And technically you defeated a second Valinhall Incarnation.”<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
